title: Phase III complete — Phase IV implementation ready to start
description: All Phase III design deliverables completed and signed off. 42 ADRs decided covering every architectural concern. 29 detailed module design documents written for all phases. 11 flow and state machine diagrams produced. 33 epic backlog files with full user stories including cross-cutting concerns (RBAC, audit logging, OTel, rate limiting, Problem Details, integration tests, graceful shutdown). 5 open design gaps resolved (hardware provider routing, runner assembly loading, air-gap bundle format, inventory storage, runner distribution). Phase IV implementation gate is open.

title: CI/CD pipeline wired across all 42 repositories
description: cloudsmith-workflows v1.0.0 published — 7 shared reusable GitHub Actions workflows (build-test, pack-publish, docker-build-push, trivy-scan, github-release, openapi-publish, dco-check). All 42 public repos seeded with class-appropriate ci.yml files and governance files (Apache 2.0 license, security policy, DCO contributing guide, code of conduct, CODEOWNERS, PR template).

title: 7 Phase V+ overlay module repositories created
description: cloudsmith-msp, cloudsmith-security-compliance, cloudsmith-governance, cloudsmith-finops, cloudsmith-bcdr, cloudsmith-itsm, and cloudsmith-siem repositories created. All public, branch-protected, and seeded with governance files and module-class CI workflows. Full design documents and epic backlogs written for each.

title: ADR-031 through ADR-041 decided — full architecture coverage
description: 11 new architecture decisions finalized: frontend state management (Zustand + TanStack Query), portal auth token handling (MSAL.js + PKCE), caching strategy (IDistributedCache, secrets never cached), audit logging (append-only PostgreSQL table), background jobs (Hangfire), rate limiting (per-tenant sliding window), database multi-tenancy (PostgreSQL RLS), testing strategy (xUnit + Testcontainers + Playwright), configuration management (IOptions<T> hierarchy), error handling (RFC 9457 Problem Details), and notification routing (INotificationProvider). Total ADRs decided: 42.

title: All 42 GitHub repositories created and protected
description: Complete polyrepo structure standing: 29 code repositories (Phase IV through VII+), 7 Phase V+ overlay repositories, 5 supporting repositories (blog, docs, infra, status, support), cloudsmith-workflows, and cloudsmith-internal. All public repositories have branch protection (no force-push, no deletion), squash-merge-only policy, and labeled issue management.

title: Phase III architecture and UX design deliverables complete
description: All 12 Phase III deliverables shipped: UX design system, 11 API surface documents, 10 portal wireflow documents, repo scaffold spec, CI/CD design (5 documents), ADR-026 versioning (Option C: semantic per-repo), data model (6 schema files), SDK contracts, security design (5 documents + trust boundaries diagram), C4 architecture diagrams (5 levels), observability design (6 documents + OTel tracing diagram). ADR-025 (RBAC), ADR-026 (versioning), ADR-027 (observability), and ADR-029 (standalone container runtime — Hyper-V Linux VM + Docker CE) all decided.

title: Monitoring Architecture — ADR-016 finalized
description: Azure Monitor Workspace (AMW) selected for PaaS metrics. DCR transformation pipeline designed with multi-destination fan-out (Analytics, Auxiliary, Alerts). VictoriaMetrics for standalone on-prem. Log collection + syslog forwarding (cloudsmith-siem-forwarder) for on-prem security event export.

title: Image Library Architecture — ADR-013 finalized
description: Self-hosted image catalog (like SCVMM) confirmed as the primary design. Operators upload ISOs and VHDs directly. Optional Azure connectors (Marketplace, Compute Gallery, Storage Account, Image Builder) provide additional import paths when Azure connectivity is available.

title: Secrets Management — ADR-009 finalized
description: Azure Key Vault for PaaS and hybrid deployments. PostgreSQL-encrypted secrets provider (AES-256-GCM, DPAPI master key) for standalone on-premises — no new services required at MVP. OpenBao available as optional production-hardening path.

title: Module Catalog expanded to 43 modules
description: The CloudSmith module catalog grew from 11 to 43 entries across 8 categories: Platform Core, Security & Governance, Lifecycle, Workload, Hardware, Overlay, Integration, and External Surfaces. Phase IV MVP scope confirmed: core + identity + secrets + inventory + cluster-mgmt + deploy + planning.

title: Web & API framework decisions — ADR-019 and ADR-020
description: React + TypeScript + Vite selected for the portal SPA (ADR-019). .NET 9 Minimal API selected for the REST API layer (ADR-020). Module-contributed UI slots and API routes enable clean extension points for community modules.

title: Four Phase A research documents completed
description: Lifecycle Framework Comparison, Module Connection Model, Persona-Capability Trace, and Deployment Model Feasibility research documents completed. Establishes the research foundation for Phase B catalog rewrite and full design phase.

title: ADR-003: Apache 2.0 License — DECIDED
description: CloudSmith will be released under the Apache 2.0 license. No contributor license agreement required beyond a DCO sign-off. Copyleft-free for commercial and MSP use.

title: ADR-001/002: Project management and repository topology — DECIDED
description: Azure DevOps with agile sprints for project management. Monorepo-per-layer topology: cloudsmith-core, cloudsmith-portal, cloudsmith-api, cloudsmith-modules, cloudsmith-runners, cloudsmith-deploy, cloudsmith-docs — each an independent repo in the cloudsmith-cloud GitHub org.

title: Phase I research complete — 11 research documents
description: Completed research on: database strategy (PostgreSQL), storage strategy, hosting (Azure Container Apps), hardware vendor APIs (Dell iDRAC, Lenovo XCC, HPE iLO, DataOn), competitive landscape (SCVMM, vCenter, ManageIQ, Nutanix), and CloudSmith domain design.

title: Project kickoff — Phase I begins
description: CloudSmith architecture and planning phase started. ADR-000 (project name, cloudsmith.cloud domain) decided. GitHub org created at github.com/cloudsmith-cloud. Architecture Decision Records process established.
